name: Klue Competitive Intelligence API
what_it_does: AI-powered competitive intelligence platform with REST API — aggregating competitor signals from web, news, and product data, generating battlecards for sales teams, tracking win/loss patterns, and delivering competitive insights via Slack, CRM, and sales tools integrations.
use_cases: Querying competitor battlecard content for CRM or sales tool embedding via API
Syncing win/loss data from CRM to Klue for competitive analysis
Pulling competitor alert feeds for Slack or sales notification workflows
Automating battlecard distribution to new sales reps during onboarding
Integrating Klue competitive insights into revenue intelligence platforms
not_for: Primary market research and primary data collection
Customer feedback and VOC programs (use dedicated survey/NPS tools)
SMB companies without dedicated competitive intelligence function
best_when: A mid-market or enterprise sales team needs systematically curated competitive battlecards, win/loss tracking, and automated competitor monitoring to arm sales reps at the moment they need it.
avoid_when: You have no competitive landscape or a very small sales team that can maintain competitive intel manually.

auth.notes: API key from Klue settings. Bearer token auth. Company-scoped access. Webhook for competitor signal events. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Partner API access may require additional agreement.

pricing.notes: Enterprise-only pricing. Canadian company (Vancouver, BC). Founded 2015. Raised $100M+. Strong enterprise adoption (HubSpot, Shopify, Zendesk as customers). AI-powered automated card generation differentiator. Slack integration for real-time alerts.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for boards, cards, and insights. Bearer token auth. Webhook for signals. Limited public API documentation — primarily partner-facing. Battlecard content may be HTML/rich text requiring parsing. Win/loss data requires CRM integration setup.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Card reads idempotent. Insight reads idempotent. Win/loss record creation idempotent by deal ID. Signal ingestion idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: API documentation is limited — primarily built for partner integrations
Battlecard content is rich text/HTML — requires parsing for plain text extraction
Win/loss data requires CRM integration configured before API access
Competitor signal freshness varies — some data may be cached
Company-level API access means all battlecards are accessible — no card-level auth

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. Canadian data residency. Bearer token with company-level scope. Competitive intelligence data is sensitive — secure API key storage critical. Partner agreements may include data handling requirements.
